heading: Depositions by agents touching depredations
label: 230 Depositions by agents touching depredations
text: The superintendents, agents, and subagents within their respective districts are authorized and empowered to take depositions of witnesses touching any depredations, within the purview of sections 227, 228 and 229 of this title, and to administer oaths to the deponents.1See References in Text note below.1
notes: References in TextSections 227 and 228 of this title, referred to in text, were repealed by , , and are covered by , Crimes and Criminal Procedure.act June 25, 1948, ch. 645, § 2162 Stat. 862section 1160 of Title 18
CodificationR.S. § 2157 derived from , .act June 30, 1834, ch. 161, § 184 Stat. 732
Indian AgentsThe services of Indian agents have been dispensed with. See note set out under .section 64 of this title

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of functions of other officers, employees, and agencies of Department of the Interior, with certain exceptions, to Secretary of the Interior, with power to delegate, see Reorg.
